dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal requires context-dependent directional bias. Entry uses RSI crossing threshold (45 for longs, 55 for shorts) as momentum confirmation, volume ratio for above-average volume, and price vs SMA_20 for trend establishment. Exit triggers on RSI falling below 40 (longs) or rising above 60 (shorts) per overshooting definition. Max hold of 10 bars enforces time-based diffusion exit. 50% retracement condition not directly translatable to allowed indicators; RSI threshold serves as proxy for momentum exhaustion. Higher timeframe opposition check cannot be expressed in single-timeframe rule.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal requires context-dependent directional bias. Entry uses RSI crossing threshold (45 for longs, 55 for shorts) as momentum confirmation, volume ratio for above-average volume, and price vs SMA_20 for trend establishment. Exit triggers on RSI falling below 40 (longs) or rising above 60 (shorts) per overshooting definition. Max hold of 10 bars enforces time-based diffusion exit. 50% retracement condition not directly translatable to allowed indicators; RSI threshold serves as proxy for momentum exhaustion. Higher timeframe opposition check cannot be expressed in single-timeframe rule.

source_rule_text: A trader should enter a momentum trade when price trend signals are early and incomplete diffusion suggests continuation, but must exit before full news diffusion causes overshooting reversal.
